title: Breviglandium palustre
intro: Breviglandium palustre is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae, commonly found in wetland areas. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as potential antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities. The bioactive constituents of Breviglandium palustre include iridoids, such as gentiopicroside, and other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include extracts, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and infections.
